title: Civic education in polarized times [Book Review]
author: Stitzlein

opening_move.description: The review opens by situating the collection against the 'pressing problem of polarization' and the increasing importance of civic education to address it.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Polarization threatens democratic life; civic education is both more important and more difficult in this context

theoretical_positioning.description: The review evaluates the collection's contributions, endorsing the dialogue format and Driver's approach while raising Morton's critique about imaginative limitation.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading and evaluation of contributors' proposals for civic education
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre. The collection itself discusses qualitative and historical evidence about civic education; the review's own argument is evaluative-normative.

case_type.case_name: Driver's student constitutional rights approach; Beaumont and Beerbohm (eds.), Civic Education in Polarized Times (2024)

confidence_notes: Book review genre. Normative mode classified as normative_with_qualitative_empirical to flag that the broader collection draws on qualitative/historical evidence about polarisation and education, even though this review is primarily evaluative.
